skill.name: zarr-python
skill.description: Guidance for building chunked N-dimensional array workflows with Zarr Python, including compression, stores, cloud storage, and NumPy, Dask, and Xarray integration.
skill.summary: Build efficient Zarr Python storage workflows for large scientific arrays.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, sensitive, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found they are markdown documentation examples and reference links. No executable scripts, prompt injection text, credential handling, data exfiltration, or malicious instructions were found in the reviewed files. The skill is safe to publish with low risk because users may copy install commands or cloud storage examples from the documentation.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Blocks Trigger Command Heuristics
low_findings.description: The command execution findings are false positives from markdown code fences and inline API names. The examples document Python and shell usage for Zarr, but the skill does not contain executable automation or hidden command runners.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are fenced markdown examples or inline function names, not code executed by the skill. No script file or runtime command dispatcher was found.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs Are Benign Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The network findings are hardcoded links to official or related documentation resources. They do not initiate network requests or send local data anywhere.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in an Additional Resources section. They are plain markdown links and there is no executable fetch, curl, webhook, or telemetry behavior.

low_findings.title: Sensitive and Weak Crypto Patterns Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The sensitive-file and weak-crypto findings are false positives from ordinary words in scientific data examples, metadata examples, and compatibility text. No certificate, key material, hashing routine, or cryptographic decision was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited sections are Zarr group, attribute, and workflow documentation. The reviewed context does not include cryptographic APIs, secrets, credentials, or certificate files.

content.value_statement: Large scientific arrays are hard to store, move, and process efficiently. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design chunked Zarr workflows for local and cloud data.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to create, open, resize, append, and index Zarr arrays.
Guides chunk size choices for different access patterns and performance goals.
Shows local, memory, zip, S3, and Google Cloud storage patterns.
Documents integration patterns for NumPy, Dask, and Xarray workflows.
Covers compressors, filters, sharding, consolidated metadata, and synchronization concerns.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for slow cloud reads and concurrent write conflicts.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not execute or validate Zarr code directly.
It does not replace official Zarr documentation for version-specific API changes.
Cloud examples require users to configure credentials and permissions separately.
Performance advice must be tested against each dataset, network, and storage backend.

use_cases.title: Design Climate Data Storage
use_cases.description: Plan chunk shapes, metadata, and cloud storage for large multidimensional climate datasets.

use_cases.title: Modernize HDF5 Workflows
use_cases.description: Translate familiar group and dataset patterns into Zarr stores with chunked access.

use_cases.title: Tune Distributed Analysis
use_cases.description: Use Dask and Xarray patterns to process Zarr datasets larger than memory.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Store
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a local Zarr array for a 10000 by 10000 float32 dataset. Recommend chunks and explain each choice.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Chunk Sizes
prompt_templates.prompt: I read my dataset mostly by time slices and sometimes by region. Recommend a Zarr chunking strategy and explain tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Cloud Access
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Zarr workflow for S3 with consolidated metadata, Dask reads, and safe write practices for multiple workers.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my proposed Zarr, Dask, and Xarray pipeline for performance risks, metadata issues, and concurrency problems.

output_examples.input: User asks for a chunking plan for a large temperature dataset.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends chunk dimensions aligned to common time and spatial queries, explains memory impact, and lists validation checks.

output_examples.input: User asks how to move an Xarray workflow to cloud-hosted Zarr.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines store selection, consolidated metadata, lazy loading, credential setup, and testing steps.

output_examples.input: User asks why cloud reads are slow.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies small chunks, missing consolidated metadata, and excessive metadata requests as likely causes.

best_practices: Choose chunks from real read and write patterns, then benchmark with representative data.
Use consolidated metadata and larger chunks when reading Zarr stores from cloud object storage.
Keep attributes JSON-serializable and document units, coordinates, and processing versions.
anti_patterns: Do not load an entire large Zarr array into memory unless the dataset is known to fit.
Do not use very small chunks for cloud storage because metadata and request overhead can dominate.
Do not allow multiple writers to update overlapping chunks without synchronization or partitioning.

faq.question: Does this skill run Zarr Python code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for users who run code in their own environment.

faq.question: Can it help with S3 or Google Cloud Storage?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains storage patterns, but users must configure credentials and permissions themselves.

faq.question: Does it support Xarray and Dask workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes patterns for lazy reads, parallel computation, and writing datasets to Zarr.

faq.question: Can it choose the best chunk size automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can recommend chunking strategies, but final choices require benchmarking with real workloads.

faq.question: Is this only for scientific data?
faq.answer: No. It is most useful for scientific arrays, but the storage patterns apply to many large N-dimensional datasets.

faq.question: What are the main risks when using this skill?
faq.answer: Users may copy install commands or cloud examples without adapting credentials, permissions, and performance settings.
